preview_text:
The model favors Portugal at 66% to win, placing them as clear favorites on a neutral ground. Uzbekistan's win probability stands at 10%, a substantial gap that reflects the model's view of an asymmetric matchup. Portugal's advantage is pronounced but not overwhelming; a one-in-four chance of a draw keeps the contest from appearing a foregone conclusion.

The spread across outcomes shows Portugal heavily favored but with meaningful room for either a draw or an upset. A draw at 25% remains the second-likeliest outcome, suggesting the model does not project a runaway result. The 56-percentage-point gap between Portugal's win probability and Uzbekistan's indicates a match tilted decisively one direction, yet the combined 35% probability of either a draw or Uzbekistan win reflects residual competitive uncertainty in the forecast.

The market consensus is considerably more confident in Portugal than the model. The sportsbooks strip to 76% for Portugal—more than 10 percentage points higher—while pricing the draw at 16% and Uzbekistan at 8%. Polymarket prices track closer to the books than to the model across all three outcomes. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is more accurate; the divergence is material enough to note.
